title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap.

content.text: Archaeology, (0) ______ is both a science and a humanity, plays a critical role in our understanding of history. The discipline involves the study of human activity through recovery and analysis of material culture. (9) ______ it may seem purely technical, archaeology also requires interpretative skills. Early archaeologists (10) ______ assumed that artifacts only needed to be catalogued. However, they have since realized (11) ______ analyzing the context in which artifacts are found is essential.|| With advances in technology, archaeologists (12) ______ now able to use methods like radiocarbon dating, which (13) ______ made it easier to determine the age of discoveries. The integration of technology means that they can achieve results (14) ______ were not possible in the past. Moreover, archaeologists increasingly collaborate with other disciplines, (15) ______ as anthropology or geology, (16) ______ enriches their findings and interpretations.

example.text: Archaeology, (0) ______ is both a science and a humanity, plays a critical role in our understanding of history.
example.explanation: Relative pronoun introducing a defining relative clause.

questions.explanation: Introduces a clause explaining the importance.
Auxiliary verb required in present continuous.
Auxiliary verb for present perfect tense.
Used to give an example of disciplines.
Relative pronoun introducing a clause about enriching findings.
